payload.description: Offset bus lanes (one lane away from the curb), SBS bus bulb stations, neckdowns and widened medians at station locations, pedestrian neckdowns, median pedestrian refuge, left-turn bays, consistent 3 lanes of traffic

evidence_refs.source_quote: All standard SBS features • Bus lanes and 3 lanes of general traffic in each direction • Changes to left-turns where needed for traffic flow and safety • Transit Signal Priority / optimized signal timings • Pedestrian safety enhancements
A smaller capital project that uses the existing SBS toolbox within the existing roadway configuration
Offset bus lanes and SBS bus bulbs
Neckdowns and widened medians at station locations
Consistent 3 lanes of traffic

issues: evidence_refs[2].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.description: Boulevard roadway design with priority for bus travel in main roadway, calmed service roads for local access and parking, curbside bus lanes in mainline roadway, SBS stations and local bus stops on expanded median, shortened crossing distance with pedestrian refuges, slip openings, left-turn bays, separates local and thru traffic

evidence_refs.source_quote: Boulevard roadway design with priority for bus travel in the main roadway and uses service roads to separate local and through traffic

Calmed service roads with parking Curbside bus lanes in the mainline roadway SBS stations and Local bus stops on side median Shortened crossing distance with pedestrian refuges Separates local and thru traffic
Separates local and thru traffic • 3 lanes total (1 lane in service road and 2 lanes in main road)

payload.description: Center-running bus lanes and median stations separate general traffic into northbound and southbound roadways, median busway with passing lane, options for physical separation/raised lanes, median bus stations for SBS and local buses, pedestrian neckdowns, rush hour parking restrictions, left-turn only signal required to cross busway, consistent 3 lanes of traffic

evidence_refs.source_quote: Center-running bus lanes and median stations separate general traffic into northbound and southbound roadways
Median bus lanes with passing lanes; options for physical separation / raised lanes • No conflicts with general traffic
Pedestrian neckdowns
Consistent 3 lanes of traffic • Rush hour parking restrictions • Left-turn only signal required to cross busway
